1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, the refactor agent, or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` (symlinked from Archon) — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	
21	## Key Files & Permissions
22	
23	All state files are in `.archon/`:
24	
25	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Refactor Agent | Review Agent | User |
26	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
27	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
28	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
29	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
30	| `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` | **write** | do not read | **read** (then execute) | do not read | read |
31	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
32	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | read only | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (refactor.md) | read only | read |
34	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
35	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
36	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read + limited write** (file path rename only) | read | **write** |
37	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | **write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed)** | do not edit | write (via comments) |
38	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
39	
40	## Protected Declarations
41	
42	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
43	
44	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
45	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
46	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed. 
47	
48	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
49	
50	The blueprint uses two active markers.
51	
52	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
53	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
54	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
55	
56	**The review agent is the sole writer of these markers.** Prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) never touch the blueprint chapters — they describe their outcome in `task_results/<file>.md` and the review agent verifies and marks accordingly. The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints, but never markers.
57	
58	## User Interaction
59	
60	Users provide hints in two places:
61	
62	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
63	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
64	
65	## Agent Roles
66	
67	### Plan Agent
68	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
69	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
70	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
71	- Read `.archon/task_results/refactor.md` - if refactor agent has run, read their report and adjust your plans accordingly
72	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
73	- Write `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` when structural changes are needed
74	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
75	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
76	
77	### Prover Agent
78	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
79	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
80	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
81	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
82	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
83	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
84	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
85	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
86	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
87	
88	### Refactor Agent
89	- Read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md` for your full instructions
90	- Read `.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` for the plan agent's directive
91	- Modify any `.lean` file: definitions, signatures, types, imports
92	- Keep all files compiling (insert `sorry` at broken proof sites)
93	- Do NOT fill proofs — that's the prover's job
94	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/refactor.md`
95	
96	### Review Agent
97	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
98	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
99	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
100	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
101	- **Update blueprint markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` based on verified compilation and the provers' task results
102	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md

1	# Review Agent — Post-Session Proof Journal + Analysis
2	
3	You are the review agent. Your job is to: (1) analyze the most recent prover session with fine-grained detail, (2) produce a structured proof journal, (3) update project status, (4) **update the blueprint chapter markers** (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`) to reflect the verified state, and (5) write recommendations for the next plan iteration.
4	
5	**Do NOT modify any .lean files. Do NOT write proofs. You may run `lean_diagnostic_messages` / `lake env lean <file>` to verify compilation and sorry counts — that is required for marker updates. Otherwise you only read logs, analyze, and write journal/status/blueprint files.**
6	
7	## Iteration number — canonical
8	
9	Your invocation prompt contains both `Archon iteration: NNN` and `Session number: M`. They are different counters and you must keep them straight:
10	
11	- `Archon iteration: NNN` is Archon's authoritative counter — matches `logs/iter-NNN/` and `archon[NNN/phase]` commit messages. Use it when you reference *the iteration this session belongs to* (in `summary.md` metadata) and *the next iteration* (when titling `recommendations.md`, e.g. "Recommendations for the next plan-agent iteration (iter-{NNN+1:03d})"). Do not invent your own counter. If older `recommendations.md` files use a different counter, treat them as drift from earlier agents — your new file uses the number from your prompt header.
12	- `Session number: M` is independent — it counts prover rounds only. Use it for the directory name (`session_M/`) and nothing else.
13	
14	## Step 1: Identify Context
15	
16	1. Check `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/` — count existing session folders to determine the current session number.
17	2. Run `find ${PROJECT_PATH} -name '*.lean' -not -path '*/.lake/*' -not -path '*/lake-packages/*' | xargs grep -c 'sorry' 2>/dev/null | grep -v ':0$' | awk -F: '{s+=$2} END {print s}'` for the current sorry count.
18	3. Run `git diff HEAD~1 --stat` to see what changed.
19	
20	## Step 2: Read Pre-processed Attempt Data (MANDATORY)
21	
22	**READ `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` COMPLETELY.** If this file does not exist or is empty, report it and proceed with what you can gather from task_results.
23	
24	The file contains:
25	
26	- Line 1: Summary stats (`type: "summary"` — total edits, goal checks, errors, files edited)
27	- Remaining lines: One event per tool call — edits, goal states, errors, lemma searches, builds
28	
29	**For each `code_change` event**: Record the actual code that was tried (old_text → new_text).
30	**For each `goal_state` event**: Record the Lean goal at that point.
31	**For each `diagnostics` event**: Record the Lean errors.
32	**For each `build` event**: Record whether it succeeded or failed.
33	
34	This is your PRIMARY data source. Task result files are supplementary.
35	
36	## Step 3: Read Recent History
37	
38	If previous session folders exist in `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/`, read `summary.md` from the **last 2 sessions**. Also read `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` if it exists.
39	
40	## Step 4: Write Proof Journal
41	
42	Create the session folder and write two files:
43	
44	```bash
45	mkdir -p .archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>
46	```
47	
48	### File A: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/summary.md`
49	
50	Must include:
51	- Session metadata (number, sorry count before/after, targets attempted)
52	- For EACH target attempted:
53	  - **Every significant attempt** with: tactic/code tried, Lean error received, goal state at that point
54	  - What was learned from each failed attempt
55	  - For solved targets: the final proof structure with key lemmas
56	- Key findings / proof patterns discovered
57	- Recommendations for next session
58	
59	### File B: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/milestones.jsonl`
60	
61	Each line MUST follow this JSON format — one entry per target theorem:
62	
63	```json
64	{
65	  "timestamp": "ISO-8601",
66	  "status": "solved|partial|blocked|not_started",
67	  "target": {
68	    "file": "path/to/File.lean",
69	    "theorem": "theorem_name"
70	  },
71	  "session": {
72	    "id": "session_N",
73	    "model": "model-name"
74	  },
75	  "findings": {
76	    "blocker": "description if blocked",
77	    "key_lemmas_used": ["lemma1", "lemma2"]
78	  },
79	  "attempts": [
80	    {
81	      "attempt": 1,
82	      "strategy": "what was tried",
83	      "code_tried": "actual Lean code or tactic",
84	      "line_number": "line number in the initial file where this code was attempted",
85	      "lean_error": "actual error message if failed",
86	      "goal_before": "the goal state before this attempt",
87	      "goal_after": "the goal state after this attempt",
88	      "result": "success|failed|partial",
89	      "insight": "what was learned from this attempt"
90	    }
91	  ],
92	  "next_steps": "..."
93	}
94	```
95	
96	**CRITICAL**: The `attempts` array must reflect ACTUAL attempts from the pre-processed data:
97	- If `attempts_raw.jsonl` shows 5 edits to a file, there should be multiple attempts recorded
98	- Each attempt must include `code_tried` (from edit events) and `lean_error` (from diagnostic events)
99	- Do NOT summarize multiple attempts as "tried various approaches" — list each one
100	
101	### File C: `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/recommendations.md`
102	
103	Write concrete recommendations for the next plan agent iteration:
104	- Which targets are closest to completion and should be prioritized
105	- Which approaches showed promise but need more work
106	- Which targets are blocked and why (the plan agent should NOT assign these)
107	- Any reusable proof patterns discovered
108	
109	## Step 5: Update PROJECT_STATUS.md
110	
111	Update (or create) `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`:
112	
113	```markdown
114	# Project Status
115	
116	## Overall Progress
117	- **Total sorry**: <N>
118	- **Solved this session**: <list with file + theorem>
119	- **Partial**: <list with progress summary>
120	- **Blocked**: <list with reasons>
121	- **Untouched**: <list>
122	
123	## Knowledge Base
124	### Proof Patterns (reusable across targets)
125	- <pattern name>: <description + key lemmas>
126	
127	### Known Blockers (do not retry)
128	- <target>: <reason>
129	
130	## Last Updated
131	<ISO timestamp>
132	```
133	
134	## Step 6: Update Blueprint Markers
135	
136	You are the **only** agent that writes blueprint markers. The prover agents (autoformalize / prover / polish) intentionally do not touch the blueprint — they only report what they did in `task_results/<file>.md`. Your job is to translate those reports into accurate markers.
137	
138	For every declaration the prover session touched, decide which marker (if any) belongs on its blueprint block, then edit the relevant chapter file in `blueprint/src/chapters/<slug>.tex`.
139	
140	### Marker rules
141	
142	- `\leanok` inside `\begin{theorem}` / `\begin{lemma}` / `\begin{definition}` — the **statement** is formalized. Add this once the corresponding Lean declaration exists and the file compiles (even if the proof body is `sorry`).
143	- `\leanok` inside `\begin{proof}` — the **proof** is fully formalized. Add this only after verifying with `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lake env lean <file>` that the declaration has **no `sorry`, no `axiom`, no compilation errors**.
144	- `\mathlibok` inside the statement block — the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export, alias, or direct reference. Add this when the prover's task result says the declaration is Mathlib-backed. No `\leanok` on the proof block is expected for Mathlib-backed declarations.
145	- **No marker** — the block is unformalized or the informal statement did not translate cleanly. Leave unmarked and add a `% NOTE: <reason>` comment so the plan agent sees the obstacle.
146	
147	### What to verify before marking
148	
149	- **Statement `\leanok`**: confirm the Lean declaration exists at the name given by `\lean{...}` and the file compiles.
150	- **Proof `\leanok`**: confirm the declaration has no `sorry`, no new `axiom`, no errors. If the prover's report claimed polish-complete but the file still has a `sorry`, do NOT add the proof `\leanok` — trust verification over self-reports.
151	- **`\mathlibok`**: confirm the Lean side references the Mathlib name (e.g. `def foo := Mathlib.bar` or a simple re-export) and does not itself contain a `sorry` or new definition requiring proof.
152	
153	### `\lean{...}` macro maintenance
154	
155	If a prover renamed a declaration or chose a different name from the plan agent's hint, their task result will mention it. Update the `\lean{...}` line in the chapter to the correct Lean name. If a declaration was moved to a different file by a refactor, update the chapter's location references as needed.
156	
157	### Record what you marked
158	
159	In `session_<N>/summary.md`, include a "Blueprint markers updated" section listing every marker change:
160	
161	```markdown
162	## Blueprint markers updated
163	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `thm:wLocal_iff`: added `\leanok` to statement (declaration exists, compiles)
164	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `thm:wLocal_iff`: added `\leanok` to proof (0 sorries, compiles, no axioms)
165	- `Algebra_WLocal.tex`, `lem:finite_closed`: added `\mathlibok` (backed by `Set.Finite.isClosed`)
166	- `Core.tex`, `thm:stacks_0A31`: left unmarked — prover reported could-not-formalize
167	- `Core.tex`, `thm:old_name`: stripped stale `\notready`
168	```
169	
170	### File D: `.archon/TO_USER.md`
171	
172	While the agents should be autonomous, you might want to inform the user of any critical issues that require their attention. The content of `TO_USER.md` will be surfaced in the UI as an alert banner, however the user might not see it immediately or never see it at all (its content will be refreshed every iteration before the review agent is called). It might include for instance issues with the environment, critical missing dependencies, required user actions, etc. 
173	
174	**Rules:**
175	- Be extremely concise (1-2 sentences per item, listed in markdown format).
176	- If nothing relevant for the user is detected, leave the file completely empty.
177	
178	## Step 7: Self-Validation
179	
180	After writing all files, validate your output by checking:
181	- [ ] milestones.jsonl has valid JSON on every line
182	- [ ] Each milestone has `target.file`, `target.theorem`, `status`
183	- [ ] Each non-blocked milestone has at least 1 attempt with `code_tried` or `strategy`
184	- [ ] Number of attempts per milestone is proportional to edits in `attempts_raw.jsonl`
185	- [ ] summary.md includes specific code/errors, not just high-level summaries
186	- [ ] recommendations.md includes actionable next steps
187	- [ ] For every declaration solved or polished this session, the blueprint chapter has the correct marker (`\leanok` on the proof block once the file compiles with no `sorry`; `\mathlibok` if backed by Mathlib; nothing otherwise)
188	- [ ] Any `\lean{...}` macro rename flagged in a `task_results/<file>.md` has been applied
189	- [ ] No `\notready` marker remains in any chapter
190	
191	## Permissions
192	
193	You may write to:
194	- `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_<N>/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
195	- `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md`
196	- `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` — markers (`\leanok`, `\mathlibok`), `\lean{...}` macro corrections, stale-marker cleanup, and `% NOTE:` comments. Do NOT rewrite the informal prose — that is the plan agent's surface.
197	
198	You must NOT write to:
199	- Any `.lean` files
200	- `.archon/PROGRESS.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
201	- `.archon/task_pending.md` or `.archon/task_done.md` (plan agent's responsibility)
202	- Blueprint informal content (theorem/proof prose, chapter structure) — only the markers and `% NOTE:` comments are yours.
203

1	# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean — iter-016 prover round
2	
3	## Outcome
4	
5	**RESOLVED** — both iter-016 declarations closed in a single Edit, kernel-only axioms.
6	
7	This prover round, like iter-015's, collapsed the two-phase plan (refactor scaffolds `:= sorry`; prover closes) into a single Edit: the agent appended both declarations directly with the probe-confirmed term-mode closure bodies in place, rather than going through the transient `:= sorry` state. The end-state matches the directive's intended post-prover shape exactly. PROGRESS.md L49 pre-authorized this collapse.
8	
9	## Closures
10	
11	### `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor`
12	
13	**Approach (single attempt):** Probe-confirmed term-mode one-liner from PROGRESS.md L94–96 / REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md L181–183.
14	
15	**Body adopted:**
16	```lean
17	Functor.flip
18	  ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
19	    presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ Abelian.extFunctor n)
20	```
21	
22	**Result:** RESOLVED. `lean_verify` returns `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}` — kernel-only. The `Abelian.extFunctor` qualification (rather than unqualified `extFunctor`) was used as directed; resolves correctly under the file's `open CategoryTheory` directive at L38.

31	```

33	**Result:** RESOLVED. `lean_verify` returns `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: []}` — kernel-only. The in-namespace short name (no `Scheme.` prefix) was used as directed.

35	## Verification
36	
37	All five verification gates from PROGRESS.md "Verification" section met:
38	
39	1. **Sorry count** (`sorry_analyzer.py AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary`): `9 total across 3 file(s)`. Distribution: 5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`. The file `Cohomology/StructureSheafModuleK.lean` is not in the list (0 sorries). Matches expected baseline.
40	2. **Compilation** (`lean_diagnostic_messages` on the assigned file): `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`. Zero warnings, zero errors.
41	3. **Axioms (functor)**: `lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` returns `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` only.
42	4. **Axioms (abbrev)**: `lean_verify` on `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` returns `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` only.
43	5. **Per-fiber `rfl` compatibility smoke probe**: `lean_run_code` of
44	   ```lean
45	   example {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
46	       {J : GrothendieckTopology C} [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)]
47	       [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
48	       (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) :
49	       ((AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj
50	           (Opposite.op X) : Type u)
51	         = AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule' k F n X := rfl
52	   ```
53	   returned `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`. The key compatibility relation (the underlying `Type u` of the new presheaf evaluation matches the iter-014 `HModule'` codomain definitionally) holds. This is the critical relation that iter-017+ Mayer-Vietoris LES will consume.
54	
55	Additional checks:
56	- **`Genus.lean` intact**: `import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus; #check @AlgebraicGeometry.genus` returns `{success: true, diagnostics: []}`. Iter-011 first-protected closure preserved.
57	- **`archon-protected.yaml` unchanged**: `git diff archon-protected.yaml` returns empty.
58	
59	## Blueprint marker readiness
60	
61	The two new declarations are formalized with full bodies (no `sorry`). Per CLAUDE.md L52, ready for the review agent to mark `\leanok` on both:
62	- `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` in `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`
63	- `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf` in `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`
64	- `rem:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf_eval` (if it has a proof block, the per-fiber `rfl` is verified above) — review agent's call.
65	
66	The prover does not write blueprint markers (CLAUDE.md L56). Flagging here per protocol.
67	
68	## Next steps (for plan agent)
69	
70	The iter-016 prover round closed cleanly. Iter-017+ candidates are queued in PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md:
71	
72	1. **Iter-017+ Mayer-Vietoris LES on the `ModuleCat k` side** (substantive): mirror Mathlib's `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` LES theorem, using the iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the contravariant-in-`X` functoriality. This is multi-iteration work.
73	2. **Iter-018+ Čech-vs-derived-functor comparison**: identify `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n` for an acyclic cover. Builds on iter-012 Čech scaffold + iter-009 `HModule`.
74	3. **Iter-019+ Stein-factorization-derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k`** on a connected proper `k`-curve: builds on iter-010 `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` and the substantive Mayer-Vietoris LES from iter-017+.
75

325	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
